name: Canning Beets (Whole, Cubed, or Sliced)
recipeIngredient: An average of 21 pounds (without tops) for a 7-quart canner load
An average of 13-1/2 pounds for a 9-pint canner load
A bushel (without tops) weighs 52 pounds and yields 15 to 20 quarts, averaging 3 pounds per quart
recipeInstructions:
1. Quality: Beets with a diameter of 1 to 2 inches are preferred for whole packs.
- Larger beets than 3 inches in diameter may be fibrous.
2. Procedure:
- Trim beet tops, leaving an inch of stem and roots to reduce bleeding of color.
- Scrub well.
- Cover with boiling water.
- Boil until skins slip off easily (about 15 to 25 minutes depending on size).
- Cool, remove skins, and trim off stems and roots. Leave baby beets whole.
- Cut medium or large beets into 1/2-inch cubes or slices. Halve or quarter very large slices.
3. Add 1 teaspoon of salt per quart to the jar, if desired.
4. Fill jars with hot beets and fresh hot water, leaving 1-inch headspace.
5. Adjust lids and process as follows:
- Table 1 (Dial-Gauge Canner):
Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
Process Time: 30 minutes for Pints; 35 minutes for Quarts
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb.
- 2,001 - 4,000 ft: 12 lb.
- 4,001 - 6,000 ft: 13 lb.
- 6,001 - 8,000 ft: 14 lb.
- Table 2 (Weighted-Gauge Canner):
Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
Process Time: 30 minutes for Pints; 35 minutes for Quarts
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb.
- Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb.
